extra.message: User input from request.POST['first_name'] is base64-decoded and passed directly to exec(). An attacker can send arbitrary base64-encoded Python code and have it executed on the server. This is a direct remote code execution vulnerability.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input from request.GET['p'] is joined with a base path using os.path.join() without any sanitization, and the result is passed to open(). An attacker can use path traversal sequences (e.g., '../../etc/passwd') to read arbitrary files on the server.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-controlled input from request.GET['url'] is passed directly to Django's redirect() without any validation or whitelist check. An attacker can craft a URL like /redirect?url=https://evil.com to redirect victims to arbitrary external sites, enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-controlled input from request.GET['fwd'] is used to look up a function name in globals() and then call it with the request object. An attacker can invoke any globally accessible view function (e.g., 'admin') without authentication, bypassing access controls entirely.

The missing_access_control view grants access to an admin page based solely on a GET parameter (?action=admin) with no authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can access the admin page by appending ?action=admin to the URL.

The dor_user_profile view fetches and allows modification of user profiles keyed by userid from the URL path without any authentication or ownership check. Any user can read or modify any other user's profile (including admin accounts) by guessing or enumerating user IDs.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The csrf_image view is decorated with @csrf_exempt, disabling Django's CSRF protection for this endpoint. Combined with the globally disabled CsrfViewMiddleware in settings.py, this endpoint accepts forged cross-site requests.

User-controlled query parameter 'qs' is rendered unescaped using the |safe filter inside a JavaScript string literal. An attacker can break out of the string context (e.g., with ";alert(1);//) and inject arbitrary JavaScript, resulting in stored or reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled query parameter 'qs' is rendered unescaped using the |safe filter in an HTML input element's value attribute. An attacker can break out of the attribute context and inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript, resulting in reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled URL path variable 'path' is rendered unescaped using the |safe filter inside a JavaScript string literal. An attacker can break out of the string context and inject arbitrary JavaScript via a crafted URL path segment.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled query parameter 'qs' is rendered unescaped using the |safe filter inside a JavaScript string literal in the CSRF image template. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript via the qs query parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: DEBUG = True is set in the Django settings file. In debug mode, Django displays full stack traces and local variable values in error pages, exposing sensitive internal application state and configuration details to any user who triggers an error.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The Django CsrfViewMiddleware is commented out in MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES. This disables CSRF protection globally for the entire application, making every state-changing endpoint vulnerable to cross-site request forgery attacks.

The XFrameOptionsMiddleware is commented out in MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES. Without this middleware, the application does not send X-Frame-Options headers, leaving all pages vulnerable to clickjacking attacks where an attacker can embed the site in an iframe.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: The Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded directly in settings.py as a plaintext string. This key is used for cryptographic signing of sessions, CSRF tokens, and other security-sensitive operations. Committing it to source control allows anyone with repository access to forge signed values.

The Django Debug Toolbar can be enabled by any user by passing a 'debug' parameter in GET, POST, or cookies. When enabled, it exposes SQL queries, HTTP headers, request/response data, template context, and settings to the requesting user, leaking sensitive application internals.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
